info:
Japanese does not have a direct equivalent of the future perfect tense. Future actions and their completion are usually indicated through context and time expressions.

Formation and Usage: The future tense and specific time expressions indicate the sequence and completion of actions.
Example: "彼が来るまでに私は食べ終わっているでしょう" (Kare ga kuru made ni watashi wa tabe owatte iru deshou - By the time he comes, I will have finished eating).
native_answer: By the end of the day, she will have gotten a gift for her friend.

audio_dir: 今日の終わりまでに、彼女は友達のためにプレゼントを手に入れているでしょう。
